title: Returning Values 📤
description: Get answers back from your functions.

slides.title: Sending Answers Back 📤
slides.content:
So far, our functions have just printed things to the screen using `void`.

But usually, we want a function to calculate some complex math and **hand the answer back** so main() can use it later.

slides.title: Return Types
slides.content:
To return a value, we swap the word `void` for a data type (like `int` or `float`).

Inside the function, we use the **`return`** keyword to shoot the value back to whoever called it.

quizData.question: If you write `float calculateTax(int amount)`, what type of data will that function hand back?
quizData.explanation: The word directly before the function name (`float`) declares what type of data the `return` statement must send back.

slides.title: The End of the Line 🛑
slides.content:
The `return` keyword is extremely powerful.

The absolute moment a function hits a `return` statement, **the function dies instantly**. Any code written below a `return` statement will never execute!

quizData.question: What happens to a `printf` statement if it is written on the line directly BELOW a `return` statement inside a function?

options.text: It is completely ignored and never runs.

quizData.explanation: `return` immediately ejects execution out of the function entirely. Nothing below it runs.
